page: Amazon Web Services m8gb.24xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by the AWS Graviton ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 processor. It features 96 dedicated physical cores operating at 2.8 GHz with a 1:1 thread-to-core ratio, meaning hyperthreading is not utilized. The system is equipped with 384.0 GB of system memory, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It operates on the Nitro hypervisor and features a 200 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. There is no local NVMe storage or GPU hardware bundled with this instance type.

In benchmark evaluations, the m8gb.24xlarge demonstrates top-tier multi-core capabilities. The stress-ng multi-core CPU test and Geekbench multi-core scores both rank in the top 10% of tested servers. Memory bandwidth is exceptionally high, with read-only small block cached memory bandwidth reaching 11,084.77 GB/sec and uncached large block write bandwidth at 242.02 GB/sec. Database operations and Redis SET operations also achieve top-tier performance, with Redis handling 195.16 million operations per second. However, single-core performance is more moderate, with stress-ng single-core and Geekbench single-core scores placing in the average to strong categories.

The absence of local storage requires reliance on network-attached storage, which may impact I/O-heavy workloads. Additionally, the ARM64 architecture lacks x86 instruction sets such as AVX or AMX, though it supports hardware-accelerated AES cryptography. This instance is highly efficient for multi-threaded workloads, including large-scale database operations, static web serving, software compilation, and medium-to-large LLM text generation. It offers a balanced resource profile, delivering high multi-core compute density and memory bandwidth without the overhead of specialized GPU hardware.
description: Amazon Web Services m8gb.24xlarge is an ARM64 general-purpose instance powered by a 96-core AWS Graviton ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 processor running at 2.8 GHz. It features 384.0 GB of RAM, providing a balanced 4.0 GB per core, and runs on the Nitro hypervisor with a 200 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. The instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. In benchmarks, the m8gb.24xlarge delivers top-tier multi-core CPU performance, memory bandwidth, and database operations, though single-core metrics remain in the average to strong range. The ARM64 architecture supports AES cryptography but lacks x86-specific vector extensions. This server is optimized for highly parallelized workloads, including multi-threaded database operations, static web serving, software compilation, and text generation for medium and large language models.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m8gb.24xlarge is a 96-core ARM64 general-purpose instance with 384 GB RAM and 200 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering top-tier multi-core and memory bandwidth performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m8gb.24xlarge is a 96-core ARM64 general-purpose instance featuring 384 GB RAM and top-tier multi-core benchmark performance.
tagline: A dedicated 96-core ARM64 instance delivering top-tier multi-core performance and high memory bandwidth for parallelized enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by a 96-core AWS Graviton ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 processor at 2.8 GHz
Equipped with 384.0 GB of system memory and 200 Gbps network bandwidth
Top-tier multi-core CPU, memory bandwidth, and database benchmark results
No local storage or GPU hardware included
Best suited for multi-threaded databases, web serving, and software compilation
